v3Philosophy.theme: Notes become first-class knowledge citizens: organized into typed collections, connected to the entity graph, and discoverable through semantic clustering and hierarchical tags.
v3Philosophy.completedPhases: Phases 1-7 (S161-S211) COMPLETE. See archive/prd-v3-s161-s207.json and archive/prd-v3-s208-s211.json.

stories.title: Entity injection at index time -- chunk vocabulary bridge
stories.summary: Inject canonical entity names (GLiNER + EntityDisambiguator) into chunk FTS5 + vector embedding text at ingest. Bridges question-vs-answer vocabulary gap on book corpora. Reindex CLI: uv run python -m app.scripts.reindex_entities.

stories.title: Graph-augmented deterministic query expansion -- replace HyDE default
stories.description:
CONTEXT: S212 iter 4 introduced HyDE-style query expansion using llama3.2:3b. Result: +15pp HR@5 on book_odyssey, +3pp on book_time_machine, but with ~7pp run-to-run variance because the LLM hallucinates source-foreign vocabulary that biases retrieval (iter 4-6 documented this). HyDE is now optional behind a flag and not used by default. Replacement: deterministic, local-first query expansion that detects entities in the user's query and expands them via Kuzu's existing alias graph. No LLM in query path -> no hallucination, no variance, no I-16 risk. Pairs with S224 -- index-side and query-side both speak the same canonical-entity vocabulary.

USER JOURNEY: Developer issues /search 'What did Mr. Holmes say to the inspector?' -> retriever runs GLiNER on the query, resolves 'Mr. Holmes' to canonical 'sherlock holmes', looks up Kuzu for aliases ('Holmes', 'Sherlock Holmes', 'Mr. Holmes', 'detective Holmes'), constructs expanded query 'What did Mr. Holmes say to the inspector? sherlock holmes Holmes' for vector + BM25 retrieval. Top-5 now contains chunks that originally referenced only 'Holmes' or 'the detective'. Eval runs without --hyde show HR@5 lift over iter 7 baseline.

CROSS-STORY IMPACT: Removes HyDE from the default retrieve() path; HyDE flag remains for opt-in comparison. Foundation for S212 closure together with S224 and S226.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- New helper backend/app/services/retriever.py::_graph_expand(query) -> str. Returns query unchanged if no entities detected or graph unreachable (fail-soft, matching the HyDE pattern).
- Use existing GLiNER service to detect entities in the query (PERSON, ORGANIZATION, PLACE, CONCEPT primarily). Resolve via EntityDisambiguator to canonical labels.
- Look up Kuzu: for each canonical entity, fetch up to 5 alias surface forms. Per I-3: guard get_next() with has_next().
- Expanded query format: '<original_query> <canonical1> <alias1a> <alias1b> <canonical2> ...'. Cap total appended tokens at 30 to bound BM25 dilution.
- New keyword-only flag graph_expand: bool = True on HybridRetriever.retrieve. Default ON. Hyde flag remains, default OFF.
- /search router gains '?graph_expand=true|false' query param (default true) so eval ablation can isolate the lever.
- Per I-2: Kuzu calls (synchronous) wrapped in asyncio.to_thread when called from async context.
- Per I-5: lazy-import gliner_service / entity_disambiguator inside the helper to avoid retriever <-> services circular imports.
- Eval: re-run book_alice / book_time_machine / book_odyssey with default flags (graph_expand=true, hyde=false). Record results in docs/exec-plans/active/S225.md vs iter 7 baseline AND vs S224 baseline (so the contribution of each lever is visible).
- Logging: structured log per query with {entities_detected, aliases_added, expanded_query_tokens}.
acceptanceCriteria: HybridRetriever.retrieve gains keyword-only flag graph_expand: bool = True (default ON)
/search endpoint accepts ?graph_expand=true|false query param (default true)
_graph_expand(query) detects entities via GLiNER, resolves via EntityDisambiguator, fetches up to 5 aliases per entity from Kuzu, returns expanded query
Fail-soft: returns original query unchanged when GLiNER finds no entities or when Kuzu is unreachable
Total appended tokens capped at 30
Per I-3: every Kuzu get_next() guarded by has_next()
Per I-5: gliner / disambiguator imports are lazy inside the helper
HyDE retains its --hyde flag (opt-in) but is not used by default; default eval runs use graph_expand only
After S224 + S225, book_time_machine HR@5 >= 0.50 on a default eval run; before/after recorded in docs/exec-plans/active/S225.md
After S224 + S225, book_odyssey HR@5 >= 0.50 on a default eval run; before/after recorded in docs/exec-plans/active/S225.md
book_alice HR@5 does not regress more than 5 percentage points vs iter 7 baseline

Integration test: retrieve a query referencing a canonical name returns chunks that only contained surface-form variants

stories.title: Multi-hint golden schema -- context_hint accepts list of alternates
stories.description:
CONTEXT: S212 iter 6 documented a class of failures retrieval cannot fix: the source has TWO parallel passages of the same fact (Odyssey BOOK II third-person AND BOOK XIX first-person of Penelope's web; Time Machine has gravitation argument re-stated by both narrator and Filby). The golden hint quotes one passage; retrieval surfaces the other; HR@5 fails despite both chunks being correct. Schema-level fix identified in iter 6: context_hint accepts either a string (current) OR a list of strings (alternates); pass HR@5 if ANY hint matches any top-K chunk.

USER JOURNEY: Curator edits evals/golden/book_odyssey.jsonl, replaces 'context_hint': 'her name was Penelope and she was weaving' with 'context_hint': ['her name was Penelope and she was weaving', 'I was weaving day and night, and unravelling']. Re-running the eval scores HR@5 hit if either substring appears in any top-5 chunk. Existing string hints continue working unchanged.

CROSS-STORY IMPACT: Pairs with S213's evals/lib/ refactor -- the schema change lives in evals/lib/schemas.py::RetrievalGoldenEntry once S213 lands. If S226 lands first, place the schema change directly in run_eval.py's GoldenEntry dataclass and revisit during S213. Closes the residual AC2/AC4 gap on book corpora that S224+S225 retrieval improvements cannot solve alone.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- Schema: context_hint: str | list[str]. Pydantic validator coerces a bare str into a single-element list internally so downstream code only handles list.
- Loader: load_golden() validates each entry; if context_hint is empty list, raise.
- Hit-detection in compute_hit_rate_5 / compute_mrr: a chunk counts as a hit if ANY hint in the list is found as a substring (case-insensitive, first-80-chars window per existing convention) of the chunk text.
- compute_mrr returns reciprocal rank of the FIRST top-K chunk that matches ANY hint.
- audit_golden.py: when reporting missed hints, list which alternate(s) were searched and which (if any) were found anywhere in candidates beyond top-K.
- Curate: for each currently-failing entry on book_time_machine and book_odyssey where iter 6/7 documented a parallel-passage cause, add at least one alternate hint. Limit additions to entries where the alternate is genuinely a parallel passage of the same fact -- do not invent hints to game the metric. Document per-entry rationale in docs/exec-plans/active/S226.md.
- Backwards-compat: existing JSONL files with str hints continue to load; no migration of valid entries required.
- Tests:
  - Unit: schema accepts str, accepts list[str], rejects empty list and non-str elements
  - Unit: compute_hit_rate_5 returns hit when any of three alternates matches
  - Unit: compute_mrr returns rank of FIRST top-K chunk that matches ANY hint
  - Integration: realign_hints.py recognizes list-form hints (already operator-side; safe extension)
acceptanceCriteria: GoldenEntry / RetrievalGoldenEntry schema accepts context_hint as str OR list[str]
Pydantic validator coerces str into single-element list internally; downstream code handles only list[str]

compute_mrr returns reciprocal rank of the FIRST top-K chunk that matches ANY hint
audit_golden.py reports per-alternate hit/miss

At least 5 entries in book_time_machine.jsonl and 5 in book_odyssey.jsonl gain alternate hints, each justified in docs/exec-plans/active/S226.md as a genuine parallel passage

stories.title: Diagnose and repair retrieval golden baseline (book HR@5=0)
stories.description:
CONTEXT: scores_history.jsonl shows the book corpus dataset returning HR@5 ~ 0.00 and MRR ~ 0.00 in the most recent runs (2026-04-20). 70 historical eval runs all show passed=false. Either the eval pipeline is broken (e.g. context_hint values do not align with current chunking) or hybrid retrieval has regressed. Until baseline is restored, no other eval story is trustworthy because we cannot distinguish 'new metric working' from 'infrastructure broken'.

2026-04-29 UPDATE: Iterations 2-7 fixed manifest, /search document_id scoping, audit tooling, hint surgery, and tested HyDE + cross-encoder rerank. Current iter 7 baseline: book_alice HR@5=0.85 PASS, book_time_machine HR@5=0.50, book_odyssey HR@5=0.30. Closure of AC2/AC4 is gated on S224 (entity injection at index), S225 (graph-augmented query expansion), and S226 (multi-hint goldens). Thresholds relaxed from HR@5>=0.60 / MRR>=0.45 to HR@5>=0.50 / MRR>=0.35 per user decision -- can be tightened later once S224-S226 land and we have headroom.

USER JOURNEY: Developer runs `cd evals && uv run python run_eval.py --dataset book_alice --backend-url http://localhost:7820 --assert-thresholds` -> exits 0 with HR@5 >= 0.50. Same for book_time_machine and book_odyssey. scores_history.jsonl reflects healthy baseline.

CROSS-STORY IMPACT: Depends on S224, S225, S226 landing first. Foundation for S213-S223 -- no other eval story is meaningful until baseline is healthy.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- evals/audit_golden.py exists (added in iter 1-2). Permanent operator tool.
- After S224+S225+S226 land, re-run all three book datasets; assert HR@5 >= 0.50, MRR >= 0.35 on each.
- Document final root-cause chain in docs/exec-plans/active/S212.md so future regressions can be diagnosed faster.

stories.title: Unified eval harness + Pydantic golden schema (evals/lib/)
stories.description:
CONTEXT: evals/run_eval.py is monolithic and only handles retrieval. Future stories add at least four new eval kinds (citation grounding, summary correctness, flashcard correctness, intent routing). They share infrastructure (golden loading, scoring history, threshold gates, results storage) but have different metrics. Refactor before adding more is cheaper than rewrite later.

USER JOURNEY: Backend/CLI only. Developer imports `from evals.lib import RetrievalEval, GenerationEval, ClassifierEval, load_golden, GoldenEntry` and writes a 30-line dataset runner instead of duplicating run_eval.py. Schema validation catches bad golden entries at load time with a clear error message.

CROSS-STORY IMPACT: S212 (audit script can use lib helpers); S226 (multi-hint schema lives here); foundation for S214-S223.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- New package evals/lib/ with __init__.py exporting public symbols.
- evals/lib/schemas.py: GoldenEntry pydantic base model (question, ground_truth_answer, context_hint, source_file, optional metadata dict). Subclasses RetrievalGoldenEntry, SummaryGoldenEntry, FlashcardGoldenEntry, IntentGoldenEntry with required-field validation. RetrievalGoldenEntry.context_hint accepts str | list[str] per S226.
- evals/lib/loader.py: load_golden(dataset_name, schema) -> list[Model]; ValidationError raises with file path + line number on bad entry.
- evals/lib/manifest.py: extract manifest helpers (load/save/ensure_ingested/lookup) from run_eval.py.
- evals/lib/scoring_history.py: append_history(dataset, model, metrics, passed, eval_kind); persists to scores_history.jsonl with new eval_kind field (default 'retrieval' for backwards-compat).
- evals/lib/store.py: store_results(backend_url, dataset, model, eval_kind, metrics) -> POSTs to /monitoring/evals/store with eval_kind.
- evals/lib/runners.py: RetrievalEval, GenerationEval, ClassifierEval base classes with .run(samples) -> metrics, .assert_thresholds(metrics, thresholds) -> raises if violated.
- evals/lib/retrieval_metrics.py: compute_hit_rate_5, compute_mrr (moved from run_eval.py with re-export for backwards-compat). Both honor list-form context_hint per S226.
- Refactor run_eval.py to use the lib; CLI behaviour unchanged.
- New CLI command `uv run python -m evals.lib.audit` validates all 7 golden files and prints PASS/FAIL per file.
- /monitoring/evals/store endpoint accepts an optional eval_kind field (default 'retrieval'); stores it on EvalResultModel.
- Tests: backend/tests/test_evals_lib.py (schema validation, loader, scoring_history append).
acceptanceCriteria: evals/lib/ package exists with schemas.py, loader.py, manifest.py, scoring_history.py, store.py, runners.py, retrieval_metrics.py

stories.title: Faithfulness + answer-relevance gates with local Ollama judge default
stories.description:
CONTEXT: RAGAS faithfulness/answer_relevancy/context_precision/context_recall are wired in run_eval.py but only run when --model is passed. They have never produced a recorded score in scores_history.jsonl. Per I-16 (local-first), enable them by default with a local Ollama judge so generation quality is measured continuously without external API egress.

USER JOURNEY: Developer runs `uv run python run_eval.py --dataset book_alice` (no flags) -> faithfulness and answer_relevance scores populated. Threshold gate (faithfulness >= 0.65) enforced when --assert-thresholds is set. Override judge via `--judge-model openai/gpt-4o` for occasional higher-fidelity runs.

CROSS-STORY IMPACT: S213 (uses lib primitives); foundation for S215, S216, S217, S220.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- run_eval.py gains --judge-model flag. Default: get_settings().LITELLM_DEFAULT_MODEL (expected ollama/* per I-16).
- Separate from --model (controls QA generation model). --judge-model controls the LLM scoring the output.
- Add --max-questions N flag with deterministic sampling (random.Random(42).sample) for fast runs.
- RAGAS evaluate() wrapped in evals.lib.runners.GenerationEval with consistent error handling. Graceful skip + clear log message if Ollama unreachable; eval still finishes with retrieval-only metrics.
- LiteLLM-compatible judge: ragas.llms.LangchainLLMWrapper around ChatOllama; no external API key required.
- Threshold enforcement when --assert-thresholds set: faithfulness >= 0.65 AND answer_relevance >= 0.50.
- /monitoring/evals/store records faithfulness + answer_relevance + context_precision + context_recall (already in schema).
- ragas + datasets pinned in evals/pyproject.toml.
- Tests: mocked-judge unit test asserts faithfulness flows from RAGAS into the metrics dict; one slow test against live judge marked @pytest.mark.slow.
acceptanceCriteria: uv run python run_eval.py --dataset book_alice (no --model flag) runs RAGAS scoring against a local Ollama judge by default

stories.title: Citation grounding eval (citation_support_rate)
stories.description:
CONTEXT: /qa returns citations -- chunks of text said to support the answer. There is no current check that a citation actually supports the claim it backs. RAGAS faithfulness measures whether the answer overall is supported by the retrieved context, not whether each cited chunk supports the specific claim attached to it. Citation grounding catches per-citation hallucination.

USER JOURNEY: Developer runs `uv run python run_eval.py --dataset book_alice --check-citations` -> for each /qa response, parse claims and citations, run an LLM judge per (claim, citation_chunk) pair returning {verdict: yes|no|partial}. Aggregate to citation_support_rate; persist; threshold >= 0.80 when --assert-thresholds.

CROSS-STORY IMPACT: S213 (lib), S214 (judge LLM infrastructure).

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- New evals/lib/citation_metrics.py: parse_claims_with_citations(answer_text) extracts (claim_text, citation_index) tuples from answers like 'X happened [1]. Y happened [2].'; judge_citation(claim, chunk, judge_model) calls LiteLLM with strict JSON output {verdict: yes|no|partial}.
- run_eval.py gains --check-citations flag; after /qa response, judge each citation against the claim it supports.
- Aggregation: citation_support_rate = (yes_count + 0.5 * partial_count) / total_citations.
- /monitoring/evals/store schema gains citation_support_rate float field (nullable).
- Tests: mocked-judge unit test for aggregation; parse_claims unit test.
acceptanceCriteria: run_eval.py --check-citations flag triggers citation grounding evaluation against the LLM judge
Each citation in /qa response is judged against the claim it supports via LiteLLM call returning {verdict: yes|no|partial}
citation_support_rate = (yes + 0.5*partial) / total computed correctly
citation_support_rate persisted to scores_history.jsonl and POST /monitoring/evals/store

stories.title: Summarization correctness eval framework (executive/detailed/one_sentence)
stories.description:
CONTEXT: summarizer (executive/detailed/one_sentence modes) and section_summarizer have zero correctness coverage. Bugs like S82 (Gutenberg metadata polluting executive summaries) ship undetected because there is no eval. Need a test that summaries cover expected themes and do not hallucinate.

USER JOURNEY: Developer runs `uv run python run_summary_eval.py --dataset summaries --mode executive` -> three metrics computed per book per mode: theme_coverage (% of expected themes present), no_hallucination (LLM judge: does the summary make claims absent from source?), conciseness_pct (length vs target). Results in scores_history.jsonl with eval_kind=summary.

CROSS-STORY IMPACT: S213 (lib), S214 (judge LLM infrastructure).

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- New evals/golden/summaries.jsonl with at least 9 entries (3 books x 3 modes): {document_filename, mode, expected_themes [list of bag-of-keywords], expected_facts [list of short claims], target_length_chars}.
- New CLI evals/run_summary_eval.py mirroring run_eval.py structure (uses evals.lib primitives).
- Pipeline: ensure_ingested(book), GET /summarize/{document_id}?mode=<mode>, score:
  - theme_coverage = count(expected_themes whose any keyword appears in summary lowercased) / len(expected_themes)
  - no_hallucination = LLM judge prompt 'Given source excerpts and summary, count claims in the summary not supported by source. Output JSON {hallucinated_count, total_claims}'; metric = 1 - hallucinated/max(total,1)
  - conciseness_pct = len(summary) / target_length_chars; ideal in [0.5, 1.5]
- Thresholds: theme_coverage >= 0.70, no_hallucination >= 0.85, conciseness_pct in [0.5, 1.5].
- Persist via /monitoring/evals/store with eval_kind=summary.
- Tests: unit test theme_coverage with synthetic samples; unit test no_hallucination with mocked judge.
acceptanceCriteria: evals/golden/summaries.jsonl exists with at least 9 entries (3 books x 3 modes)

Unit test: no_hallucination with mocked judge returning {0 hallucinated, 5 total} returns 1.0

stories.title: Flashcard correctness eval framework (factuality, atomicity, clarity)
stories.description:
CONTEXT: flashcard.py generates Q/A cards from chunks. Cards must be (a) factually correct vs the source chunk, (b) atomic (single fact), (c) clear (unambiguous question). No current eval exists. Bad cards undermine FSRS spaced repetition entirely.

USER JOURNEY: Developer runs `uv run python run_flashcard_eval.py --dataset flashcards` -> generates N cards from sampled chunks of each book; LLM judge scores each on factuality, atomicity, clarity. Aggregate metrics; threshold gates on all three.

CROSS-STORY IMPACT: S213 (lib), S214 (judge).

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- New evals/golden/flashcards.jsonl with at least 6 entries (2 chunks x 3 books): {document_filename, chunk_id_or_text, expected_card_count, expected_facts [list of short claims that should appear in cards]}.
- New CLI evals/run_flashcard_eval.py.
- Pipeline: ensure_ingested(book), POST /flashcards/generate with chunk_id, score generated cards:
  - factuality: per-card judge 'Is this card answer derivable from the source chunk? yes/no/partial'; aggregate same shape as citation_support_rate
  - atomicity: per-card judge 'Does this card test a single atomic fact? yes/no'; aggregate fraction yes
  - clarity: per-question judge 'Rate question clarity 1-5 (5=most unambiguous given the user is studying this document)'; aggregate average
- Thresholds: factuality >= 0.85, atomicity >= 0.80, clarity_avg >= 3.5.
- Persist via /monitoring/evals/store with eval_kind=flashcard.
- Tests: mocked-judge unit tests for each scorer.
acceptanceCriteria: evals/golden/flashcards.jsonl exists with at least 6 entries (2 chunks x 3 books)

stories.title: Chat-graph routing accuracy eval (intent classification)
stories.description:
CONTEXT: classify_node in the chat graph routes questions to summary | graph | comparative | search nodes. A misroute (e.g. broad book question to search instead of summary) yields poor answers. There is no eval that measures classifier accuracy. Need a labelled set of questions with expected routes plus a way to invoke the classifier without running the downstream node.

USER JOURNEY: Developer runs `uv run python run_intent_eval.py --dataset intents` -> for each {question, expected_route} pair, calls POST /qa/classify-only, compares predicted vs expected, computes routing_accuracy plus per-route precision/recall.

CROSS-STORY IMPACT: S213 (lib).

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- New evals/golden/intents.jsonl with at least 50 entries covering all 4 routes evenly.
- New internal endpoint POST /qa/classify-only that runs only classify_node + route_node and returns {chosen_route} without executing downstream nodes; saves time and avoids side effects.
- New CLI evals/run_intent_eval.py.
- Metrics: routing_accuracy = correct/total; per-route precision (correct route X / predicted route X) and recall (correct route X / actual route X).
- Threshold: routing_accuracy >= 0.85.
- Persist via /monitoring/evals/store with eval_kind=intent.
- Tests: unit tests for accuracy/precision/recall computation; FastAPI test for /qa/classify-only.
acceptanceCriteria: evals/golden/intents.jsonl exists with at least 50 entries covering all 4 routes (summary, graph, comparative, search)

stories.title: Golden dataset generation backend (LLM question gen, sizes, status polling, run trigger)
stories.description:
CONTEXT: All current golden datasets are hand-curated. Onboarding a new document means writing 10-50 questions by hand. Need an LLM-driven generator so users can create datasets from any ingested document with one click. Generated datasets must persist as queryable DB rows (not files), with status polling for the long-running generation task. Per I-16 (local-first), the generator must default to local Ollama; external LLMs allowed only by explicit opt-in.

USER JOURNEY: User (via S220 UI or curl) calls POST /evals/datasets {name, document_ids, size, generator_model?} -> backend creates GoldenDatasetModel row (status=pending) and kicks off background task. Polls GET /evals/datasets/{id}/status -> sees {status: generating, generated_count: 12, target_count: 50}. When status=complete, calls POST /evals/datasets/{id}/run to trigger eval against this dataset.

CROSS-STORY IMPACT: Foundation for S220. Reuses ingestion background-task pattern. Extends scope of run_eval.py to load datasets by id.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- New SQLAlchemy models in app/models.py: GoldenDatasetModel (id uuid str, name, description, size enum small|medium|large, generator_model, source_document_ids JSON, status enum pending|generating|complete|failed, generated_count int, target_count int, created_at, completed_at nullable, error_message nullable) and GoldenQuestionModel (id, dataset_id FK, question, ground_truth_answer, context_hint, source_chunk_id, source_document_id, quality_score float, included bool, created_at).
- DDL via index=True; indexes on (dataset_id), (status), (created_at).
- Size mapping: small = 5 q/chunk x 2 chunks/doc; medium = 10 x 5; large = 20 x 10. Hard cap 1000 questions per dataset.
- New service app/services/dataset_generator_service.py:
  - generate_dataset(name, document_ids, size, generator_model) -> creates row, schedules background task via asyncio.create_task with explicit error handler that sets status=failed + error_message
  - _generate_questions_for_chunk(chunk_text, count, model) -> LLM prompt with strict JSON output {questions: [{question, answer, context_hint}]}
  - _quality_filter(questions, source_chunk) -> drop entries where context_hint not substring of source chunk; drop entries with answers shorter than 5 chars; deduplicate via question embedding cosine > 0.95
  - Per I-1: do not share AsyncSession across asyncio.gather; use Semaphore(1) or per-task session
- Module-level get_settings() (per I-6); generator_model defaults to settings.LITELLM_DEFAULT_MODEL
- LiteLLM call site for generation; cloud models (openai/*, anthropic/*, gemini/*) allowed only when generator_model is non-default
- New router app/routers/golden_datasets.py:
  - POST /evals/datasets {name, document_ids, size, generator_model?} -> 202 with dataset id
  - GET /evals/datasets?status= -> list with metadata
  - GET /evals/datasets/{id} -> dataset + paginated questions
  - GET /evals/datasets/{id}/status -> {status, generated_count, target_count, error_message?}
  - DELETE /evals/datasets/{id} -> cascade-delete questions
  - GET /evals/datasets/{id}/golden -> list[GoldenEntry] in JSONL-shaped format consumable by run_eval.py
  - POST /evals/datasets/{id}/run {model?, judge_model?} -> kicks off run_eval as background subprocess; returns 202 with run_id
  - GET /evals/datasets/{id}/runs -> list of past eval scores against this dataset
- run_eval.py gains --dataset-id <uuid> flag; fetches via GET /evals/datasets/{id}/golden
- Tests: backend/tests/test_dataset_generator_service.py (unit, mocked LiteLLM); test_golden_datasets_router.py (FastAPI client)
acceptanceCriteria: POST /evals/datasets returns 202 with dataset id and status='pending'
Background task transitions status pending -> generating -> complete (or failed with non-empty error_message)
GET /evals/datasets/{id}/status returns live progress {status, generated_count, target_count}
Generated questions filtered: only entries where context_hint is a substring of the source chunk are persisted
Default generator_model is settings.LITELLM_DEFAULT_MODEL (local Ollama); external models require explicit non-default generator_model parameter
DELETE /evals/datasets/{id} cascade-deletes GoldenQuestionModel rows for that dataset
GET /evals/datasets/{id}/golden returns rows in JSONL-shaped format with question, ground_truth_answer, context_hint, source_file
run_eval.py --dataset-id <uuid> fetches dataset via API and runs metrics; equivalent to file-based --dataset
POST /evals/datasets/{id}/run kicks off background eval run; GET /evals/datasets/{id}/runs lists scores
Hard cap of 1000 questions per dataset enforced
Unit test: _quality_filter drops questions where context_hint not in source chunk
Unit test: size mapping returns correct (chunks_per_doc, questions_per_chunk) tuples for small/medium/large

stories.title: Evals tab UI - dataset list, Generate dialog, dataset detail, Run trigger, scores
stories.description:
CONTEXT: S219 added the dataset generation backend. The existing eval results display in the Monitoring tab is buried and has poor UX. Need a dedicated Evals tab with focused, simple UX for the full workflow: generate dataset, browse datasets, trigger eval run, view scores. Explicit user direction: simplistic and usable, not like the prior Monitoring eval surface.

USER JOURNEY: User clicks 'Evals' in top nav -> sees datasets list (cards: name, size badge, status, question count, last run HR@5/MRR, generator_model) and a primary 'Generate Dataset' button. Clicks Generate -> dialog: name + size radio (Small ~10/doc, Medium ~50/doc, Large ~200/doc) + document multi-select + generator model dropdown (local Ollama default; External options visible). Submits -> dataset appears with live 'generating 12/50' progress. When complete, clicks the dataset -> detail panel shows metadata, paginated question list (Q/A/hint, expandable), prominent 'Run Eval' button (model picker), past run scores table.

CROSS-STORY IMPACT: New top-level tab; consumes S219 endpoints. App.tsx nav extended.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- New top-level tab 'Evals' added to App.tsx nav. Order: Learning | Chat | Viz | Study | Notes | Evals | Monitoring.
- New page frontend/src/pages/Evals.tsx.
- New components in frontend/src/components/evals/:
  - DatasetCard.tsx (name, size badge, status pill with progress when generating, question count, last-run scores, model)
  - GenerateDatasetDialog.tsx (name, size radio Small/Medium/Large with help text, document multi-select via search, generator_model dropdown with explicit External-API warning when non-default)
  - DatasetDetail.tsx (shadcn Sheet side-drawer; metadata header, paginated question list, Run Eval button, past runs table)
  - QuestionList.tsx (paginated, Q + A + hint, expandable per-row)
  - RunEvalDialog.tsx (judge_model picker default Local Ollama, --check-citations toggle, --max-questions slider default 20)
  - ScoresTable.tsx (HR@5, MRR, Faithfulness, Date, Model columns)
- TanStack Query: useDatasets(status), useDataset(id), useDatasetStatus(id) with 5s polling while status='generating' (stops on transition out), useDatasetRuns(id)
- Selecting an External generator/judge model shows inline warning 'This will send chunks to an external API'
- Loading skeleton (3 placeholder cards), error inline message per panel, empty state ('No evaluation datasets yet -- click Generate Dataset to create your first one. Datasets let you measure retrieval and generation quality.')
- No MarkdownRenderer in list cells; use stripMarkdown for any inline previews (per I-12)
- Reuse shadcn Dialog, Sheet, Card, Badge, Select, Progress, Table, Button. No new deps.
acceptanceCriteria: New top-level 'Evals' tab visible in app navigation between Notes and Monitoring

stories.title: Eval regression detection + Monitoring Eval Trends panel
stories.description:
CONTEXT: scores_history.jsonl accumulates eval scores but nothing alerts on regressions. Quality drift goes undetected. Need a regression detector + UI surface that warns when key metrics drop vs a moving baseline.

USER JOURNEY: User opens Monitoring tab -> sees new 'Eval Trends' panel: line chart of HR@5 / MRR / Faithfulness over time per dataset. If any metric dropped >= 5% vs the moving baseline (last 5 runs), alert badge appears with offending dataset+metric.

CROSS-STORY IMPACT: S214 (uses persisted faithfulness/answer_relevance), S219 (datasets generated by user are also tracked).

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- New service app/services/eval_regression_service.py: detect_regressions(window=5, threshold_pct=0.05) -> list of {dataset, metric, current_value, baseline_value, drop_pct, eval_kind}
- New endpoint GET /monitoring/evals/regressions returning the list
- Frontend component frontend/src/components/EvalTrendsPanel.tsx mounted in Monitoring page
- Recharts line chart: one line per metric, dataset selector dropdown switches the chart
- Regression alert badge at top with count; click expands details
- Polls /monitoring/evals/regressions every 60s; stops on unmount
- Tests: unit test detect_regressions with synthetic history; FastAPI test for endpoint
acceptanceCriteria: GET /monitoring/evals/regressions returns list of regressions vs moving baseline (window=5, threshold=5%)

Dataset selector dropdown switches the chart between datasets

Unit test: detect_regressions with synthetic history returns expected regressions
Unit test: empty history returns empty list

stories.title: Retrieval-strategy ablation eval (vector vs FTS vs graph vs RRF)
stories.description:
CONTEXT: Hybrid retriever uses RRF over vector + FTS + graph. When HR@5 regresses, today there is no way to attribute the loss to a specific strategy. Ablation eval that runs each strategy in isolation makes regressions diagnosable.

USER JOURNEY: Developer runs `uv run python run_eval.py --dataset book_alice --ablation` -> for each query, runs vector-only, fts-only, graph-only, rrf separately; reports per-strategy HR@5/MRR side by side. Persists ablation table to scores_history.

CROSS-STORY IMPACT: S213, S214.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- Retriever interface gains a strategy parameter: get_retriever().retrieve(query, document_ids, k, strategy='rrf'|'vector'|'fts'|'graph'); rrf is default for backwards-compat
- run_eval.py --ablation flag triggers 4 separate runs per strategy
- Output prints per-strategy HR@5/MRR table
- Persist via /monitoring/evals/store with eval_kind=ablation; metrics dict carries per-strategy values
- /monitoring/evals/store schema gains ablation_metrics JSON column (nullable)
- Tests: unit test ablation flag produces 4 metric sets; mocked retriever asserting strategy param flows through
acceptanceCriteria: Retriever supports a strategy parameter (vector|fts|graph|rrf); rrf is default for backwards-compat
run_eval.py --ablation flag triggers per-strategy runs (4 strategies)
Output prints a table with HR@5/MRR per strategy
Per-strategy metrics persisted to scores_history.jsonl with eval_kind=ablation
/monitoring/evals/store accepts eval_kind=ablation and stores per-strategy ablation_metrics JSON

Unit test: retriever with strategy='vector' calls only the vector path (no FTS, no graph)

stories.title: Activate cross-domain golden datasets (paper, conversation, notes, code)
stories.description:
CONTEXT: golden/paper.jsonl, golden/conversation.jsonl, golden/notes.jsonl, golden/code.jsonl exist on disk but none are wired into manifest.json -- the eval runner cannot use them because their source documents are never ingested. Each domain stresses different parts of the pipeline (papers: dense PDF; conversation: turn-taking; notes: short-form; code: language-aware chunking).

USER JOURNEY: Developer runs `uv run python run_eval.py --dataset paper` -> backend ingests sample paper from DATA/papers/, runs eval, reports HR@5/MRR. Same for conversation, notes, code datasets. Per-dataset thresholds (papers harder than conversation).

CROSS-STORY IMPACT: S213 (lib), S214 (judge).

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- Source fixtures: add at least one sample per domain to DATA/{papers,conversations,notes,code}/ (small public-domain or hand-written samples)
- Wire each into evals/golden/manifest.json via first-run ingestion (existing ensure_ingested logic handles this if source_file is present in the golden entries)
- Per-dataset thresholds in run_eval.py (or evals.lib.runners): paper HR@5>=0.45 MRR>=0.30; conversation HR@5>=0.55 MRR>=0.40; notes HR@5>=0.60 MRR>=0.45; code HR@5>=0.50 MRR>=0.35 (relaxed in line with the 2026-04-29 S212 threshold cut; tighten later)
- VALID_DATASETS extended to include all 7 dataset names
- Tests: smoke runs against each dataset asserting at-least the threshold
acceptanceCriteria: DATA/papers/ contains at least one sample paper file (PDF or txt)

DATA/notes/ contains at least one sample notes file

Per-dataset thresholds applied (configured in THRESHOLDS or per-dataset override map)

regressionBaseline: All tests on branch ralph/luminary-v2 pass before Phase 1 implementation begins (run: uv run pytest from backend/). After each story, the test count must be >= the count at the start of that story's implementation. Any drop is a regression -- stop and fix before setting passes: true.
